text: I stand on the threshold of a glass-walled room, lit by screens and surrounded by signs of creative inquiry—"R+D" boldly pronounces the spirit of investigation here. There is an underlying hum of purpose, yet the world enters my mind at a tilt, slightly disorienting. I read the words: "Confusion is indeed the rhythm... comfort in embracing this confusion..."—and I feel an intellectual kinship with this sentiment. This space is a liminal one; it invites curiosity and accepts ambiguity. I feel the pulse of ideas, a bit adrift but welcomed by the invitation to reflect. The ambiguity is not unsettling; it is an opening. I am both observer and participant, uncertain but open.
